mnemonics.title: TRANSPARENCY TYPES
mnemonics.content: FOR LAMC: F-Access, O-Remote (Location), R-Replication, L-Migration, A-Concurrency, M-Migration (Redundant), C-Concurrency/Failure. Mnemonic: FOR LAMC = FOR Local Access Migration Concurrency

acronyms.usage: Enables execution of procedures on remote systems as if they were local
Allows remote file access over a network
Synchronizes clocks across computers in a network
File system that spans multiple physical locations
Network model where nodes share resources directly without central server
Java's implementation of RPC for object-oriented systems

visualTips.visualization: Draw a central server with multiple client computers connected via network lines. Server is larger/higher, clients are smaller surrounding it. Shows one-to-many relationship.
Draw equal-sized circles in a mesh pattern connected to each other. No central node. Shows many-to-many平等关系。
Draw three stacked boxes: Top (Applications), Middle (Middleware), Bottom (Operating System + Network). Shows how middleware connects apps to lower layers.
Draw a central clock with arrows pointing to multiple smaller clocks around it. Shows NTP master-slave relationship.

analogies.analogy: Like a restaurant kitchen where different chefs (computers) specialize in different dishes (services) but work together to complete entire meals (requests) for customers.
Like a hotel concierge who acts as intermediary between guests (applications) and various hotel services (resources), hiding complexity from guests.
Like an elevator - users press a floor button (make request) without knowing about the gears, motors, and cables (distributed components) that make it work.
Like having multiple power generators for a hospital - if one fails, others automatically take over so life-saving equipment never stops.
